name: claude-legal-immigration-laws
description: Shared reference corpus for U.S. immigration law. Snapshots the canonical rules verbatim — the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA, 8 U.S.C. Chapter 12), the immigration regulations in 8 CFR (DHS/USCIS/CBP/ICE chapter I plus the DOJ/EOIR immigration-court and Board of Immigration Appeals chapter V at 8 CFR 1001-1337), the State Department visa/passport/exchange parts of 22 CFR, and the Foreign Affairs Manual (FAM — 9 FAM 100/302/402/502/504 + 8 FAM 300 + 7 FAM 000/1400) — and indexes the immigration case-law sources for on-demand lookup: the federal circuit courts (petitions for review of removal orders under INA Sec. 242 / 8 U.S.C. Sec. 1252, via CourtListener and the connected CourtListener MCP), the Board of Immigration Appeals precedent decisions (I&N Dec., via the EOIR Virtual Law Library), and the USCIS Administrative Appeals Office (AAO) decisions. fam.state.gov omits the Sectigo intermediate from its served TLS chain (the server's own misconfiguration, not a proxy or bot-gate), so scripts/pull_fam.py AIA-chases the intermediate, adds it to a verify bundle, and crawls the site's JSON table-of-contents API for verbatim text. This is a sources/reference plugin (no drafting skills yet): statutes and regulations are mirrored as Markdown by scripts/pull_ina.py and scripts/pull_immigration_cfr.py; FAM by scripts/pull_fam.py; case law stays on-demand per legal-data-apis.md and online-sources.md.
